zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes de la Mare於1317年出生在英國維爾爾望別洛時，她的父親John de la Mere21歲，母親Mrs. Joan Delamare15歲。她於大約1330年與John de Paveley在英國維爾爾結婚。他們至少是3個女兒的父母。她去世於1361年10月5日在英國維爾爾維斯特波利享年44歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes de la Mare于1317年出生在英国維爾爾望別洛时，她的父亲John de la Mere21岁，母亲Mrs. Joan Delamare15岁。她于大约1330年与John de Paveley在英国維爾爾结婚。他们至少是3个女儿的父母。她去世于1361年10月5日在英国維爾爾維斯特波利享年44岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Mare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Americo, Cesareo, Fernando, Ines, Javier, Jose, Marcos.  Portuguese Henrique.  Italian Antonio, Gino, Olindo, Silvio, Vito.  Dutch Andries.
Italian: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mare ‘sea’.
English: variant of   Mear  .

variants.name: Marucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Silvio, Angelo, Carmela, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Guido, Licinio, Lorenzo, Luciano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Maruccio, a pet form of Mare or Mari .

variants.name: Marelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Giuseppe, Angelo, Dante.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Mare  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: English: variant of   Mear  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes de la Mare was born in 1317, in Wanborough, Wiltshire, England, her father, John de la Mere, was 21 and her mother, Mrs. Joan Delamare, was 15. She married John de Paveley about 1330, in Wiltshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 daughters. She died on 5 October 1361, in Westbury, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 44.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes de la Mare est née en 1317, à Wanborough, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, John de la Mere, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Joan Delamare, avait 15 ans. Elle a épousé John de Paveley environ 1330, à Wiltshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 5 octobre 1361, à Westbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes de la Mare 1317 in Wanborough, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John de la Mere 21 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Joan Delamare 15. Sie hat um 1330 in Wiltshire, England John de Paveley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 5. Oktober 1361 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Westbury, Wiltshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de la Mare è nata il nel 1317 nel Wanborough, Wiltshire, England, sua padre, John de la Mere, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Joan Delamare, aveva 15 anni. Lei sposato John de Paveley circa 1330, nel Wiltshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figlie. Morì il 5 ottobre 1361, nel Westbury, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 44 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes de la Mare(이)가 1317년 영국 윌츠히르 완버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John de la Mere(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Joan Delamare(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 대략 1330년 영국 윌츠히르에서 John de Paveley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 10월 5일 영국 윌츠히르 웨스트버이에서 44세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes de la Mare nasceu em 1317, em Wanborough, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de la Mere, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Joan Delamare, tinha 15 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Paveley aproximadamente 1330, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 5 de outubro de 1361, em Westbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 44 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes de la Mare родилась в 1317 г. в местности ванбороую, уилцхир, Англия, её отцу по имени John de la Mere было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Joan Delamare, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Paveley около 1330 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 дочери. Она умерла 5 октября 1361 г. в местности вестбури, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 44 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes de la Mare nació en 1317, en Wanborough, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de la Mere, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Mrs. Joan Delamare, tenía 15 años. Se casó con John de Paveley aproximadamente 1330, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijas. Murió el 5 de octubre de 1361, en Westbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años.
